title: Here’s what happened in crypto today
summary: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, Web3 and crypto regulation.

title: AI microbusinesses could drive $262B in stablecoin volume by 2033: Swyftx
summary: The AI-native cohort of the expanding gig economy could increasingly use stablecoins to avoid slow and expensive traditional payment rails, Australian crypto exchange Swyftx said.

title: Oil Jumps as US Conducts Wave of Strikes Against Iran
summary: Global benchmark Brent rose above $79 a barrel while West Texas Intermediate edged toward $75 after US forces completed another wave of strikes against Iran, with the two sides disputing whether the Strait of Hormuz was open. Bloomberg's Stephen Stapczynski reports. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: TSMC’s Sales Soar 36% in Latest Sign of AI Spending Momentum
summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. reported a 36% jump in quarterly sales, meeting elevated expectations while signaling global demand for AI computing remains intact.

title: Circle (CRCL) Wins Final OCC Approval for National Trust Bank
summary:
Bitcoin Magazine

Circle (CRCL) Wins Final OCC Approval for National Trust Bank
Circle won final OCC approval to establish a national trust bank, allowing it to place USDC reserves under federal oversight and expanding its regulated digital asset custody business.

title: How to Make Product Ads With AI for TikTok and YouTube—For (Almost) Free
summary: One product photo, three AI tools, and about 20 minutes: Here's the full workflow for generating a sales video without a camera, a model, or a studio.

title: What Is Robinhood Chain? The Ethereum Layer-2 Network for Tokenized Stocks
summary: Robinhood Chain is an Ethereum layer-2 network built with Arbitrum technology for tokenized assets, crypto apps, and on-chain financial products.

title: Bank of Thailand audits high-volume stablecoin trades to crack down on illicit finance: report
summary: The central bank and the SEC have reportedly begun using data analytics tools to examine abnormal stablecoin trades, with a focus on USDT.

title: Japan’s SBI Group to launch JPYSC stablecoin lending service this month
summary: The SBI's service would offer users a 3% annual yield for deposited JPYSC stablecoins, according to Nikkei.
